title: Vegan chocolate chip cookies
subtitle: Chewy and crisp-edged, with no eggs or dairy
excerpt: A vegan chocolate chip cookie that achieves the crisp edge and chewy centre of the butter-egg original through coconut oil and brown sugar. Mixes in 10 minutes, bakes in 12.
sourceNotes: The chocolate chip cookie was invented by Ruth Wakefield at the Toll House Inn in Massachusetts in 1938. Vegan adaptations using coconut oil and plant milk in place of butter and eggs emerged in American and British vegan baking communities from the 2000s onward. This recipe draws on the method developed by Isa Chandra Moskowitz in Veganomicon (2007) and adapted by Meera Sodha in East (2019).

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze baked cookies in a sealed bag for up to 3 months. Thaw at room temperature. Or freeze the unbaked dough in balls and bake from frozen, adding 2 minutes to the baking time.
recipe.batchNotes: Dough can be scaled up and baked in batches. Use the same weight per ball (25 g) to keep baking times consistent.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The dough can be refrigerated for up to 3 days before baking. Bake straight from cold; the cookies will spread a little less.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, one tray at a time on the middle shelf

content.text: Coconut oil behaves like cold butter at room temperature and like melted butter when liquid. For a chewy rather than cakey cookie, use it melted and cooled. Solid coconut oil creamed into sugar acts more like a shortbread fat and produces a crumblier result.
The cookies look underdone when you take them out. That is correct. They firm up as they cool on the tray. Leaving them in until they appear set will result in dry, hard cookies once cold.

Heat the oven to 180°C fan. Line two baking trays with baking paper. Whisk the cooled {{coconut-oil}} with the {{light-brown-sugar}} and {{caster-sugar}} until combined. Add the {{oat-milk}} and {{vanilla-extract}} and stir well. Add the {{plain-flour}}, {{bicarbonate-of-soda}}, and {{fine-sea-salt}} and mix until a dough forms. Fold in the {{chocolate-chips}}.

Roll into balls of about 25 g each and place on the lined trays, spaced 5 cm apart. Flatten each ball slightly with the palm of your hand. Bake for 10 to 12 minutes until the edges are set and pale golden but the centres still look soft. Leave to firm on the tray for 10 minutes before transferring to a rack.
